title: Arenaria makmelensis
intro: Arenaria makmelensis, also known as Makmal rock jasmine, is a species of flowering plant in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which contribute to its potential health benefits in treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Arenaria makmelensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the most common preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
taxonomy: Arenaria makmelensis is classified within the plant kingdom, specifically within the family Caryophyllaceae. It belongs to the genus Arenaria, which consists of various species of flowering plants. Arenaria makmelensis is further classified into the order Caryophyllales and the class Magnoliopsida. The kingdom to which it belongs is Plantae, which encompasses all plant species. Within the kingdom, it is classified as a species of flowering plant, Eudicot.
